overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong professional social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, with a combined following of 3,580. His content is highly academic and research-oriented, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. Across all platforms, he achieves high levels of engagement, particularly when announcing tangible research outputs, software releases (like CooRnet v2.0), and academic opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, software releases (CooRnet), and methodology discussions regarding coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Job openings and opportunities for PhD students and postdoctoral researchers in computational social science.
Announcements of newly published papers, grants awarded, and conference presentations.

recommendations: Capitalize on Bluesky's high engagement rate (average 6.14%) by prioritizing it for early discussions, research drafts, and interactive academic threads.
Increase LinkedIn interaction by actively replying to comments on job listings and grant announcements to trigger algorithmic boosts and expand reach.
Include direct links to papers, tool repositories (like CooRnet on GitHub), or job application portals in Mastodon posts to increase click-through rates.
Leverage visual assets, such as paper abstract figures or tool screenshots, to increase the visual appeal of posts across all platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky serves as the most interactive hub, generating the highest average engagement rate (6.14%). Research announcements and educational opportunities resonate deeply with this community, as seen by high repost counts.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides steady engagement (average 4.55% rate) from a highly niche, technical audience. Highly technical updates like the CooRnet v2.0 release perform exceptionally well here.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn hosts the largest follower base (1,850) and acts as the primary professional channel. It excels at driving high volume engagement for institutional updates, such as grants (107 engagements) and recruitment (73 engagements).

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
